steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the choice that emphasizes a similarity between Ephydra cinerea and Coccochloris. A similarity means something both organisms share.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The Great Salt Lake in Utah is one of the saltiest bodies of water in the world.': Describes the lake only — mentions neither organism. Does not address the similarity between them. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Aquatic insects called Ephydra cinerea and bacteria called Coccochloris both live in the southern portion of the Great Salt Lake.': Names both organisms and states explicitly that they both share the same location. Directly emphasizes the similarity. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Ephydra cinerea, a type of aquatic insect, lives in the Great Salt Lake.': Mentions only Ephydra cinerea — ignores Coccochloris entirely. No similarity is expressed. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Bacteria called Coccochloris live in the Great Salt Lake, which is located in Utah.': Mentions only Coccochloris — ignores Ephydra cinerea. No similarity is expressed. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
